reasoning: Human bocavirus (HBoV) is an emerging pathogen whose exact pathogenic role is still debated due to high coinfection rates. Extensive searches yielded no evidence of any HBoV vaccine in clinical trials, let alone approaching regulatory approval. A 2023 report explicitly states 'there is no vaccine in development for human use', and searching clinicaltrials.gov for 'bocavirus vaccine' returns 0 results. Given the knowledge cutoff is October 2025, it is impossible for a vaccine to go from pre-clinical (or non-existent) to approved and listed on Wikipedia by January 2026. Because of the extremely rigid, multi-year timeline of vaccine development, the probability of a vaccine being successfully developed and Wikipedia being updated to 'Yes' in the next 1 to 3 months is effectively zero aside from the possibility of Wikipedia vandalism on the resolution dates. Therefore, probabilities reflect the lowest allowable bounds, increasing slightly for more distant horizons to account for marginally more uncertainty.
